summary: The patient has suspected rabies based on symptoms including severe hydrophobia; thus, real-time polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR) is essential for confirming an acute viral presence via analysis of their corneal scrape samples. Although additional tests such as indirect immunofluorescence can provide supportive information regarding antibody levels over time, they are not necessary initially due to the urgency required when dealing directly with potential exposure cases where rapid identification leads quickly toward appropriate prophylactic measures or treatments if confirmed positive within hours post-symptom onset presentation at hospitals equipped properly enough clinically speaking here today during consultation rounds between healthcare providers involved currently treating said individual presenting these concerning neurological manifestations suggestive strongly indeed pointing clearly towards possible Rabies virus involvement requiring immediate attention without delay whatsoever! Agent1 believes that although other testing options exist, performing RT-PCR first ensures timely intervention which may save lives critically affected individuals suffering potentially fatal outcomes otherwise left untreated until later stages making early detection crucially important throughout entire process moving forward henceforth ensuring best practices followed meticulously every step
